evals.prompt: Write a commit message for the staged changes.
evals.expected_output: A commit message following the canonical format: imperative title with issue number from branch, Summary section, Changes section grouped by layer with backtick-wrapped class names.
expectations: Title starts with an imperative verb (Add, Fix, Improve, etc.) without type prefix
Title includes issue number extracted from branch name (#18)
Summary section exists with why and what bullets
Changes section groups by layer (API, Application, Domain, Infrastructure, etc.)
Class names are wrapped in backticks
Output contains ONLY the commit message, no explanation or code fences

evals.prompt: I want to amend the previous commit with additional staged changes. Write the amend commit message combining both.
evals.expected_output: A single commit message that covers both the original HEAD commit changes (form validation) and the newly staged changes (email validation, loading state), not just the delta.
expectations: Message covers both the original commit changes (form validation) AND the new staged changes (email validation, loading state)
Title starts with an imperative verb without type prefix
Output contains ONLY the commit message, no explanation or code fences
Does not describe only the new delta - describes the combined result

evals.prompt: Generate an AI commit message prompt for IntelliJ IDEA that I can paste into JetBrains IDE settings.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained prompt using JetBrains template variables ($GIT_DIFF, $GIT_BRANCH_NAME, etc.) with commit format rules embedded, ready to paste into IDE settings.
expectations: Prompt includes commit format rules (imperative mood, no type prefix, issue number)
Prompt is self-contained and ready to copy-paste
Prompt instructs to output only the commit message
